recentTweets.text:
Today’s fatal ICE shooting in Maine has raised more questions than answers.
 
I called on DHS Secretary Mullin to launch a full, fair, and transparent investigation—and I’m going to hold him to his word there will be one. https://t.co/mvKeN0Ozmq

The Fourth of July is a celebration of the revolutionary ideals set forth in the Declaration of Independence—liberty, equality, and self-government.

As America marks its 250th birthday, may we continue striving to build a more perfect union.🇺🇸 https://t.co/RXv9SVUz1j

Clean energy is becoming more and more affordable—and smart energy policy can help lower costs, strengthen American competitiveness, and create jobs.
 
It was great to join SEEC Institute’s Solutions Summit to talk about how we turn those goals into lasting results. https://t.co/o6cezGEfvc

As we approach America’s 250th birthday, it’s worth revisiting the ideas that built our country. 
In this episode of Civics in 60, I’m breaking down the Federalist Papers—the essays that helped explain & defend the Constitution at a pivotal moment in our history. https://t.co/6gAUUWU6CJ

As we approach #America250, we must ensure the next generation understands the Constitution and how our government works.

Thanks to @BrookingsInst for hosting this important bipartisan discussion on civic education. https://t.co/0rRnlb6TLy

Joshua Chamberlain was a college professor who answered his country's call—and changed the course of history.
 
His story reminds us that America has always been shaped by ordinary citizens willing to step forward when it matters most. #WeAreAmerica250 https://t.co/XMVy8vvPUv

The issue here isn't architecture. Or patriotism. It's the Constitution.
 Congress passes our nation’s laws. And no president—regardless of party—has the authority to violate three separate laws on a project that Congress hasn't authorized.
https://t.co/2KU4ZwwJPk

Veterans deserve a responsible VA—and that starts with accountability within the organization.
 
That's why I secured confirmation that VA staff who report misconduct remain protected from retaliation—despite rumors of restrictive NDAs being considered.
https://t.co/vl0VnH6Yvl

The new Rachel Carson Center for People & Nature is a reminder that conservation & education go hand in hand.

This center will help connect future generations to the outdoors while showcasing the natural beauty & wildlife that make our state so special.
https://t.co/sGoxhvmGTu

Intelligence only works when analysts evaluate the facts and tell the truth.
 
After a nominee spoke about the importance of independent analysis and the willingness to tell policymakers what they don't want to hear, his response to my follow-up question was disappointing. https://t.co/wwDAGb7jJA

Investing in first responders boosts our workforce and makes communities safer.
 
At the York County Regional Training Center, I saw how federal dollars are helping train the next generation of firefighters, EMTs, dispatchers, & law enforcement officers.
https://t.co/SO7JqyWaXX

This #MemorialDay, I hope you’ll pause for a moment and remember those who made the ultimate sacrifice for our country.
 
Celebrate with friends and family, but be mindful of the men and women who made it all possible. https://t.co/XhsQyNwnmz

I didn’t realize so many young people on the Internet knew Johnny Appleseed 🍎…

But the idea holds up: plant now, benefit later. Better health for your family and far less cost to you (and taxpayers) in the years to come. https://t.co/vF3N5rlZ5u

Prevention measures like an $11 bath mat could save Americans tens of thousands of dollars.
 
If Medicare would send these out to every recipient in America, I’ll bet the investment would pay for itself in under a year. https://t.co/7obfboWZ6A

Building American warships in South Korea or Japan is the worst idea since the Red Sox traded Babe Ruth to the Yankees.  We have the skilled workers, the shipyards, and the know-how right here in America — especially at Bath Iron Works.
 
https://t.co/Si9C8BYN6H

A reconciliation bill is a fast-track budget bill that lets the Senate pass certain spending & tax changes with a simple majority.

However, there are strict rules about how this works. That’s where something called a “Byrd Bath” comes in.

Here's a Civics in 60 to help explain⬇️ https://t.co/YvgUKKNjfJ

Maine veterans deserve timely, quality care — done in a safe and professional environment.
 
Cutting police positions at Togus and chalking it up to bureaucracy is wrong. I will continue pushing to ensure our vets receive the care and support they earned.
https://t.co/rcsVwgGdfo

In addition to saving and improving lives in Africa, USAID also helped to prevent violent extremism from expanding across the globe.
 
Financial cuts to America’s “soft” power lead to increased terrorist activity—putting Americans in danger at home and abroad. https://t.co/KQBkB1TSkm

Budgets reflect policy priorities.
 It makes no sense to cut U.S. Forest Service operations & research while spending nearly the same amount on a presidential ballroom.
 
Today, I told the USFS Director there is no justification for such a cut to caring for our nation’s forests. https://t.co/uXoYCBThgv

Modern infrastructure is essential to protect Maine community health & safeguard our natural resources.
 It was great to join the Freeport Sewer District as they broke ground on a major upgrade to improve wastewater treatment capacity for years to come.
https://t.co/9r2kHnNlSq

If “energy dominance” means $4.30/gallon for gas and nearly $6/gallon for diesel, we need a new definition.
 Or at least a different approach.
 
If we want better stability in our energy supply, America should add lower cost options like wind and solar.
 
https://t.co/z03Pfltj9y

Today’s Armed Services Committee hearing with Defense Secretary Hegseth is very important – and overdue. 

Before it began, I shared a range of things I'm looking to hear about with @KateBolduan and @NewsCentralCNN. https://t.co/ORFIJ67dxN

On this episode of Civics in 60, @HC_Richardson and I discuss King Charles III’s visit to Congress this week and his reminder of the Magna Carta’s influence on self-government, individual liberty, and the rule of law. https://t.co/0SN9Qu3Jiz

Last week, I was proud to visit @MidCoastHunger with @RepMcGovern to see the vital work they’re doing to ensure Maine families have access to healthy food.
 
Their work, along with the countless Maine people who help others, strengthens our state and communities every day. https://t.co/tMUkTV74Rc

The Second Continental Congress founded the US Postal Service to deliver the mail, period—not to be an arm of the government & definitely not an arbiter of federal elections.
 
My colleagues & I are reminding the Postmaster General of its essential role.

https://t.co/0XgFIEAWHb

As energy costs continue to rise, this administration is canceling previously approved and vetted power projects.
 
That’s not only bad for household budgets, but also, it’s incredibly difficult for energy companies that follow the rules and want to improve energy affordability. https://t.co/lLrJGupSEI

251 years ago, the first shots of the American Revolution were fired.
 
On Patriots’ Day, we remember the courage shown at Lexington & Concord—as well as the inspiring idea that ordinary citizens can shape the course of history. https://t.co/H3GYCwybYQ

Cracking down on tax cheats shouldn’t be controversial.
 
Our Stop Cheaters Act is about fairness—improving customer service, making sure everyone pays what they owe & enforcing the law across-the-board to minimize illegal evasion by high-income taxpayers

https://t.co/P1cr29lC7R

Welcome home to the brave men and women of the 136th Engineer Company.
 
After months of service in Saudi Arabia, your efforts strengthening infrastructure and supporting our partners reflects the very best of Maine.

https://t.co/3D3VmUxMMB

Too many Maine people lack access to housing, a human challenge that also exhausts ER services & correction resources.
 
This tiny home village is a win-win—providing safe housing & services for those who need it, & saving taxpayer dollars in the long-run.
https://t.co/BIJdm7gMDZ

America’s seafood industry professionals deserve access to the same financial resources and tools that our farmers get.
 
Our bill would help fishermen, processors, and mariculture businesses access the resources they need to compete and grow.

https://t.co/TOFqw1fXys

Members of the US Military swear an oath to the constitutional norms our founding fathers envisioned.
 
Today, a Pentagon overly influenced by politics is putting military integrity & the safety of Americans across the globe at risk.
 
My piece in Time: https://t.co/7cGRJK35OO

No veteran should go without care, no matter where they are. The bipartisan Get Justice Involved Veterans BACK HOME Act would expand access to mental health services for incarcerated veterans, supporting recovery, rehabilitation, & a stronger path forward.
https://t.co/w6wqzhRydR

As Governor, I approached every issue with one basic question: what’s the problem we’re trying to solve?
 
The SAVE America Act seeks to solve a problem that barely exists - voter fraud is much rarer than being hit by lightning - and instead, disenfranchises millions. https://t.co/wFhHoS8dTt

We can fund the TSA.
We can fund FEMA.
We can fund CISA.
We can fund the Coast Guard.
Then we can talk through our differences on reining in the tactics of ICE and CBP to better protect Americans.
I hope my colleagues support this approach the next time it comes up for a vote. https://t.co/RGJukuCAz0

Analysts from across the political spectrum have reviewed the data & the conclusion is clear: there is no widespread fraud in our elections.

The SAVE America Act is a solution in search of a problem. 

https://t.co/UL1IMePrjs

If anyone is “winning” America’s war in Iran, it would be Vladimir Putin. As the United States, Israel, and Iran military strikes cause the price of oil to spike, it’s meant $6 billion more in Russian oil sales. 
 
In the meantime, American troops remain in harm's way. https://t.co/jpeMFLvVit

The glory of America is our shared journey, including the hard parts, toward a more perfect union.
 
The administration's attempts to erase history, to try to keep us from understanding the historical events that shaped this country, amounts to spreading a damned lie. https://t.co/0sg8lfa5eF

Thanks to @PunchbowlNews for hosting me & @SenatorRicketts for a discussion focused on finding common ground in polarized times.
 
As former Governors, we understand the importance of compromise and reaching across the aisle to get results for Americans. https://t.co/KFO8mfHYLx

There's nothing more quintessentially Maine than wild blueberries, potatoes from the County, & crisp apples.
 
Along with the Maine delegation, we urged the USDA to deliver specialty crop assistance & help these Maine-grown industries handle adversity.
 
https://t.co/kgE05r9MJ6

Our national parks tell the story of our nation – the good, the bad, but always the TRUTH. Attempting to erase or omit our dark chapters only works to promote a repeat of these sad periods in history. 

We must continue to share the full history of our great nation. https://t.co/5gOLNsaHyE

Our founders knew the importance of checks and balances – and the solemn responsibility of putting American lives at risk abroad.

That’s why I voted in favor of @SenTimKaine’s War Powers Resolution. As the people’s representatives, only Congress has the power to declare war. https://t.co/21lRWvXHwV

The Ayatollah was a brutal, repressive leader responsible for the deaths of hundreds of Americans and thousands of Iranians. I don’t mourn him for a second.
 
BUT the Constitution gives Congress – and only Congress – the power to declare war.

More of my thoughts here ⬇️ https://t.co/zliM8yaoi2

At its core, this job is about service.
 
Helping Maine people from across the state resolve issues with federal agencies — whether it’s veterans’ benefits, Social Security, or IRS questions — is one of the most direct, meaningful ways my team and I fulfill that responsibility. https://t.co/z3IuGFWcMA

Too many Maine small businesses were harmed by Trump's unconstitutional tariffs — costing billions nationwide.
 
That’s why I cosponsored the Tariff Refund Act, which would require refunds for those tariffs & prioritize small businesses first.

https://t.co/ln9I7kXb6T

Today's students shape our future by studying American history closely, and becoming independent, critical thinkers.

I appreciated the chance to speak with @ColbyCollege students this week at a Q&A on public service, leadership, and the issues shaping our collective future. https://t.co/cWyK2EyJW5

Today, on #PresidentsDay, take advantage of free entry into our National Parks, truly “America’s Best Idea!”
 
As Theodore Roosevelt reminded us, “cherish these natural wonders, cherish the natural resources, cherish the history.” https://t.co/pHsX3N4qF5

I was honored to meet with Maine @SpecialOlympics Unified athletes and advocates this week.
 
At it’s core, athletics is all about overcoming challenges and demonstrating resilience. I’m excited to see these athletes succeed in their events — and their lives! https://t.co/rinpaz5ijK

Maine people deserve clarity on special ICE operations in Maine—what the goal is, who is being targeted, how decisions are being made, and whether or not ICE is still operating on our streets.

Rumors don’t cut it when public safety is on the line.

https://t.co/Gd0Cc02tvS

It was an honor to lead a bipartisan group of Senate colleagues and travel to Greenland. Our message was simple & direct: we have unequivocal respect for our longtime Arctic partners.

National security & global stability depend on allies —Greenland & Denmark are 2 of our best. https://t.co/1QDTVt6Lm4

When you can’t hire employees, it’s tough to operate a business.

H-2B visas are a lifeline for Maine’s small businesses who look for seasonal help & can’t get enough local applicants. I'm glad that my bipartisan push for more visas got the green light.
https://t.co/TadDYhPR9i

Many of the tactics being used in ICE’s current operations are violating the 1st, 2nd, and 4th Amendments of the Constitution.
 
The most extreme actions of these masked agents are not only unprofessional and unnecessary, they are proving to be deadly. https://t.co/te966ZQgqs

I can’t support the proposed DHS budget without adding adequate guardrails and robust accountability. I told @FaceTheNation we should pass the rest of the spending bills and negotiate a responsible path forward that avoids a government shutdown.

https://t.co/s0N7XQolIl

As Dr. Martin Luther King, Jr. famously said, "our lives begin to end the day we become silent about things that matter.”

On #MLKDay2026, let’s continue his legacy of speaking up for what matters—liberty & justice for all. https://t.co/LDwYPei9et

Deterrence is key to any successful defense strategy against cyberattacks. Making sure our foes think twice.

The fact our Cyber Command nominee couldn’t share his views on deterrence is disappointing – and doesn’t instill confidence in protecting our interests & infrastructure. https://t.co/wC5UbB68zL

We maintain national security by securing alliances - not territory.

America, Denmark, and Greenland have worked together for 75 years to protect our interests in the #Arctic. If we want to add more assets there, we can work with them and keep this NATO relationship strong. https://t.co/B5NBjbAizu

My new bill would expand an existing Maine law that requires employers to display a poster with information about veterans benefits, helping make sure veterans in the workplace know what benefits they have earned and how to access them.

https://t.co/BbSsylbS4u https://t.co/C0HVUwiqAR

There’s no national security reason for the U.S. to buy, capture, or take over Greenland. None.

We must instead continue to work with our trusted NATO allies – like we’ve done for 75 years – to protect the Arctic and our interests.

https://t.co/t7BFJsV0EU

The operation in Venezuela has essentially given our adversaries—Russia and China—hall passes to do what they want in their region of the world.

Only time will tell how this action will impact the geopolitical landscape and fuel the ambitions of our global foes. https://t.co/eKPgvppElv

While this past year has had its challenges, this season is a time to slow down and reflect with family and friends on all we have to be grateful for.

From my family to yours, Happy Holidays and may all your days be merry and bright. https://t.co/fDa6bjooDl

assessment.summary: The captured X archive has 4 Kurdistan Lens tweet hits for Angus S. King, Jr.. The social-media posture reads as constructive or diplomatic signal because the strongest matched frame is support, partnership, or official-contact language. Top matched terms: Oil / Energy.
assessment.whatItWasLike: When this account mentioned the Kurdish file, the language most often clustered around support, partnership, or official-contact language. I count 0 direct Kurdistan/Kurdish-region term hits, 0 security-frame hits, 1 supportive or diplomatic hit, 0 humanitarian/minority hits, and 4 energy/economic hits in the exported profile window. The latest matched tweet is dated 2026-07-01T13:37:01Z and should be opened before treating this as a final stance.
